objective: Complete residency intake and secure work credentials.
opposition: Nora's legalistic orientation narrows acceptable subject matter to legacy celebration.
turn: Marisol learns donor clauses can end the commission for reputational harm.
consequence_cost: She starts under institutional surveillance and self-censors opening notes.

objective: Meet Salvador in the closed gallery and request interview access.
opposition: Salvador refuses formal interview protocol and tests Marisol's nerve.
turn: He recognizes Gloria's name and offers private sessions only.
consequence_cost: Marisol trades procedural safety for exclusive access.

objective: Ground herself after the meeting without drinking.
opposition: Craving rises after Salvador's provocation and secrecy bait.
turn: Luis answers and tells her to write facts before feelings.
consequence_cost: She postpones relief and commits to evidence logging.

objective: Record Salvador's first full statement.
opposition: Pain medication and ego make him evasive and theatrical.
turn: He states Tomas did not die a martyr and claims Marisol was lied to.
consequence_cost: Her central identity story destabilizes before she can verify anything.

objective: Decide whether to suppress or pursue Salvador's claim.
opposition: Fear of relapse and fear of family betrayal pull in opposite directions.
turn: Marisol chooses investigation and alerts Luis instead of hiding.
consequence_cost: She loses emotional distance and enters the mystery personally.

objective: Use All Souls dinner to test family chronology casually.
opposition: Gloria and Carina redirect questions into ritual talk and jokes.
turn: Date inconsistencies appear around Tomas's final week.
consequence_cost: Marisol realizes the family script is coordinated, not incidental.

objective: Find physical corroboration inside Gloria's apartment.
opposition: Guilt stops Marisol from openly searching rooms.
turn: She discovers a hidden photo of Gloria and Salvador predating her birth.
consequence_cost: She crosses a privacy line and cannot return to passive trust.

objective: Get Ada to run technical scans on Salvador's triptych.
opposition: Conservation policy forbids informal data sharing.
turn: Ada agrees to supervised viewing but not file extraction.
consequence_cost: Marisol gains limited access while Ada assumes risk.

objective: Interpret underpainting evidence without overreaching.
opposition: Nora insists technical traces are not narrative license.
turn: Infrared reveals a painted-over figure marked T.V.
consequence_cost: The mystery shifts from hearsay to material alteration.

objective: Identify next evidence path from catalog metadata.
opposition: Archive indexing is incomplete and intentionally opaque.
turn: A file code points to Red Hook storage manifests.
consequence_cost: Marisol's investigation widens beyond museum-controlled spaces.

objective: Retrieve Red Hook manifests tied to T.V. entries.
opposition: Water damage and missing boxes block straightforward search.
turn: A mislabeled crate yields partial shift logs.
consequence_cost: Hours of labor produce only fragmentary proof.

objective: Validate whether logs actually refer to Tomas Vega.
opposition: Union steward stonewalls and calls her project predatory.
turn: Retired handler Benny confirms Tomas by scar description.
consequence_cost: Marisol gains witness corroboration but alienates union contacts.

objective: Assemble timeline from copied documents.
opposition: Contradictory dates make interpretation uncertain.
turn: She confirms Tomas worked the night before the alleged protest death.
consequence_cost: Family myth loses chronological plausibility.

objective: Confront Gloria with the Red Hook evidence.
opposition: Gloria intercepts Marisol outside AA and frames secrecy as maternal duty.
turn: Gloria admits Tomas died of overdose in a motel.
consequence_cost: Marisol's martyr framework collapses in public space.

objective: Prevent immediate self-destruction after the confession.
opposition: Shame and rage overwhelm coping routines.
turn: Marisol buys vodka and drinks alone in a bus shelter.
consequence_cost: She resets sobriety to day zero and hides the lapse.

objective: Inventory practical fallout from relapse day.
opposition: Hangover, missed calls, and unpaid bills compress decision-making.
turn: She learns she forfeited a paid youth workshop shift.
consequence_cost: Economic precarity sharpens the cost of one drinking episode.

objective: Re-enter recovery community honestly.
opposition: Fear of judgment pushes toward minimization.
turn: Marisol takes a day-one chip and states the relapse out loud.
consequence_cost: She regains procedural sobriety while losing self-image of control.

objective: Reconnect with Ada outside institutional context.
opposition: Both distrust mixed motives after recent secrecy.
turn: At dinner, Ada sets clear boundaries about files and emotional labor.
consequence_cost: Potential intimacy becomes conditional rather than romantic fantasy.

objective: Decide whether to disclose relapse to Ada.
opposition: Marisol fears disclosure will end trust immediately.
turn: She confesses the drinking lapse before they touch.
consequence_cost: Ada chooses cautious openness instead of withdrawal.

objective: Move from confession into physical intimacy.
opposition: Residual shame and grief make Marisol detach mid-encounter.
turn: Ada pauses and asks direct consent questions, then intimacy resumes.
consequence_cost: Their bond deepens through negotiated vulnerability, not rescue.

objective: Capture Salvador's final roof-garden testimony on tape.
opposition: Salvador mixes remorse with self-exoneration and control.
turn: He says he may be Marisol's biological father.
consequence_cost: Interview becomes personal rupture instead of archival exercise.

objective: Discharge rage without drinking.
opposition: Public platform rewards speed over reflection.
turn: Marisol posts an accusatory poem naming patterns everyone can identify.
consequence_cost: Museum crisis protocol activates and targets her position.

objective: Argue against suspension at formal review meeting.
opposition: Counsel and donors treat her as reputational threat, not artist.
turn: Badge is deactivated and stipend frozen effective immediately.
consequence_cost: She loses institutional leverage and immediate income.

objective: Preserve contact with Ada after compliance order.
opposition: HR warns Ada that informal help could end her employment.
turn: Ada sends one brief message: she needs distance for now.
consequence_cost: Marisol is isolated from both archive access and emotional support.

objective: Request certified death records for Tomas Vega.
opposition: Archive backlog and hostile gatekeeping delay processing.
turn: Clerk Imelda identifies a form error Marisol can correct same day.
consequence_cost: The search continues, but only through bureaucratic endurance.

objective: Obtain cause-of-death verification.
opposition: Official language around overdose triggers shame spiral.
turn: Certificate confirms acute fentanyl-heroin toxicity.
consequence_cost: Family myth is no longer defensible as misunderstanding.

objective: Check witness and report signatures for cross-links.
opposition: Marisol expects anonymous police documentation.
turn: Witness line reads Salvador Rivas with full address.
consequence_cost: She gains decisive evidence while deepening personal betrayal.

objective: Present documents at family dinner and demand full truth.
opposition: Gloria reframes forgery as survival under immigration fear and poverty.
turn: Carina admits partial prior knowledge and accuses Marisol of weaponizing art.
consequence_cost: Sibling trust breaks in front of Leo.

objective: Keep housing stability after confrontation.
opposition: Carina prioritizes household calm over continued conflict.
turn: Carina tells Marisol to leave that night with one suitcase.
consequence_cost: Marisol becomes temporarily unhoused and relationally exiled.

objective: Draft catalogue essay before Salvador's condition worsens.
opposition: Early drafts flatten into revenge rhetoric and self-justification.
turn: Marisol rewrites to include her relapse and Tomas's addiction detail.
consequence_cost: She trades moral clarity for harder narrative honesty.

objective: Secure final interview window with Salvador.
opposition: Nurse warns lucid intervals are brief and unpredictable.
turn: Marisol submits focused questions and gets one morning slot.
consequence_cost: Dread increases because closure is time-limited and uncertain.

objective: Spend non-strategic Saturday with Leo and Carina.
opposition: Awkwardness keeps conversation brittle and defensive.
turn: Leo's pigeon obsession pulls everyone into ordinary laughter.
consequence_cost: Family baseline reappears without resolving core conflict.

objective: Repair minimal trust with Carina through practical help.
opposition: Carina doubts Marisol can show up consistently.
turn: They complete errands and homework together without arguing evidence.
consequence_cost: Carina allows limited future contact under strict boundaries.

objective: Understand Gloria's motives beyond control.
opposition: Marisol expects another strategic lie.
turn: Pawn ticket proves Gloria sold her wedding ring for Marisol's first rehab payment.
consequence_cost: Marisol's moral frame of Gloria becomes more complex and less punitive.

objective: Obtain legal permission to use Salvador's taped confession posthumously.
opposition: Estate attorney invokes defamation and donor risk to block release.
turn: Salvador dies before signing final authorization.
consequence_cost: Official route to publication closes.

objective: Find an alternative evidentiary path.
opposition: Ada risks termination if she shares restricted conservation files.
turn: Ada sends corroborating scans and metadata through a traceable channel.
consequence_cost: Marisol gains proof while Ada is placed on administrative leave.

objective: Attend Salvador's wake and observe legacy narrative formation.
opposition: Mourners and clergy pressure everyone toward saintly coherence.
turn: Marisol hears contradictions erased in real time.
consequence_cost: She decides silence would reenact the core lie.

objective: Read a poem that refuses sanitization.
opposition: Gloria and Nora urge restraint to avoid permanent fallout.
turn: Marisol reads anyway, naming overdose, forgery, and tenderness together.
consequence_cost: Gloria walks out and family rupture becomes public.

objective: Absorb immediate aftermath of public rupture.
opposition: Local arts reporter requests comment while mourners split into camps.
turn: Nora refuses on-record condemnation and leaves without speaking.
consequence_cost: Institutional conflict remains unresolved but cannot be privatized again.

objective: Meet Gloria at Coney Island to set publication boundary.
opposition: Both arrive prepared to defend incompatible moral claims.
turn: Gloria confirms Salvador's paternity and says Tomas chose the martyr lie for benefits and Marisol's childhood stability.
consequence_cost: Marisol loses the option of simple villain framing.

objective: Choose manuscript form and disclosure limits.
opposition: Pressure from media rewards total expos\u00e9 and certainty.
turn: Marisol commits to a hybrid documentary-poetic book with redactions and consent limits.
consequence_cost: She accepts critique from all sides in exchange for ethical coherence.

objective: Read from the completed manuscript at a small bookstore six months later.
opposition: Audience skepticism and career contraction shadow the event.
turn: Marisol reads survival-centered work without martyr rhetoric.
consequence_cost: She claims an artistic identity not dependent on inherited myth.

objective: Define next step with Ada after public fallout.
opposition: Neither can promise uncomplicated trust or immediate domestic merger.
turn: They agree to restart slowly while Marisol keeps daily recovery structure.
consequence_cost: Ending lands on ongoing practice rather than closure fantasy.
